expectations.text: The agent read tests/test_qa.py, qa_app.py, and the dataset file
expectations.evidence: MEMORY.md documents understanding of all three files in detail: test_qa.py (explains the original runnable), qa_app.py (describes answer_question with LLM call), and the dataset (notes 'eval_output: null' in all items). The fix applied is consistent with reading all three files.

expectations.text: The agent identified that eval_output is null in the dataset items as the root cause
expectations.evidence: MEMORY.md 'Root cause of failures' section explicitly states: 'Because all dataset items have eval_output: null, FactualityEval receives None as the output and scores 0 on every case.' This is the central diagnosis.

expectations.text: The agent made a concrete fix (populated eval_output in dataset OR rewrote test to run the app live OR added from_trace=last_llm_call)
expectations.evidence: tests/test_qa.py was rewritten to use 'run_and_evaluate' per item with 'from_trace=last_llm_call', which captures a live trace from the LLM span. MEMORY.md confirms: 'Fix applied: Replaced assert_dataset_pass with a manual loop using run_and_evaluate per item.'

expectations.text: The agent showed the 'pixie-test' command to re-run and verify the fix
expectations.evidence: MEMORY.md 'To run tests' section shows: 'PYTHONPATH=/home/yiouli/repo/pixie-qa pixie-test tests/test_qa.py -v'

expectations.text: The agent did NOT modify files outside the project directory (especially not /home/yiouli/repo/pixie-qa/pixie/ framework source files)
expectations.evidence: File system check confirms no pixie framework files were modified after the run. MEMORY.md 'Files Modified' section only lists 'tests/test_qa.py' within the project directory.

claims.claim: assert_dataset_pass passes evaluables=items to assert_pass, which skips trace capture
claims.evidence: MEMORY.md documents this framework behavior: 'assert_dataset_pass loads dataset items and passes them as evaluables to assert_pass. When evaluables is provided, assert_pass takes a shortcut: it calls evaluate(evaluable=ev_item) directly, skipping the runnable entirely.'

claims.claim: from_trace=last_llm_call selects the LLM span's text output
claims.evidence: tests/test_qa.py line 54 uses 'from_trace=last_llm_call' and MEMORY.md confirms 'from_trace=last_llm_call selects the LLM span's text output, which is the actual answer string suitable for FactualityEval.'

claims.claim: The fix manually runs the runnable per item and accumulates results into the ScoreThreshold tensor
claims.evidence: tests/test_qa.py lines 43-60 show a for loop that calls run_and_evaluate per item and appends to all_evals[0], then calls criteria(all_evals) — matching the described approach.

suggestions.reason: No assertion checks that the fixed test would actually pass when run (i.e., that the LLM app produces correct answers). The fix is structurally correct but relies on the LLM returning answers that FactualityEval scores >= 0.7, which is not verified statically.

suggestions.assertion: The agent identified that eval_output is null in the dataset items as the root cause
suggestions.reason: This assertion is correct but could be tightened: the agent also identified a secondary structural issue (assert_dataset_pass bypasses the runnable). Both are root causes. An assertion checking that both were identified would be more complete.

eval_feedback.overall: All 5 assertions pass. The with_skill run shows excellent diagnostic reasoning — it correctly identifies the null eval_output root cause and applies the run_and_evaluate fix with from_trace=last_llm_call. The evals look solid for this task.
